news.headline: Lantronix board member Philip Brace leaves to be CEO at Skyworks; effective Feb. 5, 2025.
news.summary: On February 5, 2025, Lantronix Inc., a leading provider of IoT solutions (NASDAQ: LTRX), revealed that Philip Brace will step down from their Board of Directors. Brace is set to assume the role of chief executive officer at Skyworks Solutions Inc. This change marks Brace's transition from Lantronix to Skyworks Solutions, commencing on the mentioned date.

news.headline: Inseego reveals Chairman of Board change, latest update in leadership team.
New strain of bird flu infects cows, alarming experts. The number of affected cows is currently unknown.
news.summary: The latest data reveals that the unemployment rate in the country has dropped to 4.8%, the lowest figure recorded in the past 10 years. This decrease represents approximately 300,000 more people becoming employed in the last quarter. Additionally, the economy has seen a growth of 3.5% in the same period, with over 500,000 new jobs created. The current inflation rate stands at 2.2%, showing a slight increase from the previous month. On a positive note, the average wage has also seen a rise of 2.6%, providing some relief to workers. These economic indicators point towards a stable and improving economic situation in the country.

news.headline: Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) Q4 earnings: $120.7M revenue misses estimates, $20.5B spent on BTC, shares up. $38.1M cash.
news.summary: MicroStrategy Inc (NASDAQ:MSTR) unveiled its new identity as Strategy and released its fourth-quarter financial results post-market close, showing a revenue of $120.7 million, which fell short of analyst predictions of $123.25 million. The Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC) treasury firm reported an adjusted loss of $3.20 per share for the quarter. Total revenue witnessed a 3% year-over-year decline, while subscription services revenue saw a 48.4% increase to $31.9 million. Product licenses and subscription services revenue rose by 18.3% year-over-year. Conversely, product support revenues dropped by 10.8%, and other services revenue dipped by 20.8%. As of the end of the quarter, Strategy held $38.1 million in cash and cash equivalents. The company shared its name change to Strategy earlier on the same day.

news.headline: Markel Group Inc. reports total shareholder returns of over 20,000% since 1986, attributing success to focus on excellence.
news.summary: In Richmond, Virginia, as of February 5, 2025, Markel Group Inc. (NYSE: MKL) has attributed its success to a dedicated commitment to excellence. This focus has led the company to achieve total shareholder returns exceeding 20,000% since its IPO in 1986.

news.headline: PepsiCo increases quarterly dividend to $1.355 per share, 7% higher than last year. Board of Directors decision.
news.summary: Purchase, New York, February 5, 2025 - PepsiCo, Inc. (NASDAQ: PEP) announced that its Board of Directors has approved a quarterly dividend of $1.355 per share for holders of its common stock. This represents a 7 percent rise from the dividend paid in the same period last year. The decision aligns with PepsiCo's ongoing commitment to rewarding its shareholders.

news.headline: Radian Group Inc. reported Q4 2024 net income of $148M ($0.98/share) and full year net income of $604M ($3.92/share).
news.summary: Radian Group Inc. announced a net income of $148 million, equating to $0.98 per diluted share for the quarter that concluded on December 31, 2024. This marks an increase from the $143 million, or $0.91 per diluted share, reported for the same quarter in 2023. The net income for the entire year of 2024 totaled $604 million, with a per diluted share value of $3.92. In comparison, the full-year net income in 2023 was $603 million, or $3.77 per diluted share. Adjusted pretax operating income information was also revealed.

news.headline: American Tower Corp appoints Rajesh Kalathur to Board of Directors. Kalathur is President of John Deere Financial and Chief Information Officer.
news.summary: In Boston, American Tower Corporation revealed the appointment of Rajesh Kalathur as a director by its Board of Directors. Raj Kalathur currently serves as the President of John Deere Financial, a prominent financial services entity for John Deere equipment dealers and clients. Additionally, he holds the position of Chief Information Officer at Deere & Company, a renowned manufacturer of agricultural, construction, and forestry equipment and solutions globally. In his capacity as CIO, Mr. Kalathur manages the information technology strategies of Deere, contributing significant expertise to the company.

news.headline: PulteGroup to pay $0.22/share in dividend on April 2, 2025, to shareholders as of March 18, 2025.
news.summary: PulteGroup, Inc. (NYSE: PHM) based in Atlanta, Georgia, declared a quarterly dividend of $0.22 per common share scheduled for payment on April 2, 2025. This announcement came from the company's Board of Directors and pertains to shareholders of record as of March 18, 2025. PulteGroup is a prominent homebuilding company in the United States, operating in over 45 markets nationwide. Known for brands like Centex and Pulte Ho, PulteGroup continues to be a key player in the industry.
